name: Portuense Portuguese
notes: Porto city and Greater Porto (Gaia, Miragaia, Leça, Vitória). Diagnostic features per DIALECT_PATTERNS / whitepaper5 (TigreGotico internal dialect research): (1) BETACISM — /v/ → [b] unconditionally, a categorical phoneme merger (vinho→[ˈbiɲu], estava→[ʃˈtabɐ]); (2) /ow/ PRESERVED — Porto retains the ou diphthong where Lisbon has [o] (ouro→[ˈowɾu]); (3) /ej/ PRESERVED — ei kept as [ej] where Lisbon has [ɐj] (primeiro→[pɾiˈmejɾu]); (4) apicoalveolar sibilants [s̺, z̺] — encoded in allophones; (5) uvular variation [ʁ, χ, ʀ, x]; (6) heavy coda [ɫ]; (7) extreme unstressed vowel reduction. NOTES-ONLY (sporadic/lexical): l-palatalization (quilo→quilho), Filipa→[fʎipɐ]; Porto-style diphthongization (stressed /e/→[je], /o/→[wo]) is sub-lexical and not encoded. Sources: DIALECT_PATTERNS.md §Betacism, §Diphthongs; whitepaper5 §4.1 N1-N2, §4.4.

ancestors.notes: Standard European Portuguese; Porto diverges in betacism (v→b), diphthong preservation (ei/ou), and apicoalveolar sibilants

sources.author: Boléo, M.P.
sources.title: Estudos de linguística portuguesa e românica

sources.author: TigreGotico
sources.title: DIALECT_PATTERNS.md and whitepaper5: Phoneme-Level Dialect Transforms for European Portuguese
sources.notes: Internal dialect feature matrix and IPA transform inventory; pending external peer review

stress.notes: Acordo Ortográfico 1990 conventions: paroxytone default; written accents override; final -i/-u/-r/-l/-z/-x/-im/-om/-um/-ns and nasal diphthongs attract final stress. Unmarked -em/-am stay paroxytone (homem, falam).
